text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy little village called Greenfield, there lived an eccentric old inventor named Cornelius. He was known for his peculiar inventions and even more peculiar habits. Among his oddities was a fascination with feathers. He dreamed of flight, not just bird wise, but feather wise.

One moonlit night, tired of sitting on his rafters tinkering with gusts-of-wind gadgets, Cornelius decided to try something new. He plucked all his feathers and fashioned them into a big, fluffy cloak. Then he climbed onto his old stool.

With a flutter of his eyelashes, Cornelius waved his cloak. A gust. He cheered himself. But then another. Stronger. He bounced!

He flew! Not very far, just a few hops. But he felt free! He thought maybe he could help the birds. He spread seeds.

The next morning, Owl ears perched nearby heard about the bird food. She flew down.

"Why such feathers?" she asked
